items.correct: The release of the White House tapes proved he obstructed the FBI's Watergate inquiry; facing certain impeachment by Congress, he resigned to avoid conviction.
items.misconception: Nixon resigned because he personally ordered the Watergate break-in.
items.random_wrong: Nixon resigned because he lost the 1972 election to Carter.
items.why: Nixon's crime was the cover-up, not necessarily ordering the burglary. Students assume worse acts when the constitutional issue was actually obstruction of justice.

items.correct: Ideological incompatibility plus mutual suspicion after WWII — disputes over Eastern Europe, the atomic bomb, and broken wartime agreements.
items.misconception: Stalin started it by invading Eastern Europe after the war.
items.random_wrong: It began when the USSR shot down a US spy plane.
items.why: Students treat one side as solely responsible. Most historians now see the Cold War as a mutual breakdown — both sides acted on rational fears that the other read as aggression.

items.q: What happened in the Russian Revolution of 1917?
items.correct: There were two revolutions: February overthrew the Tsar and installed the Provisional Government; October saw the Bolsheviks seize power from that government.
items.misconception: The Bolsheviks overthrew the Tsar in 1917.
items.random_wrong: Lenin personally led the storming of the Winter Palace.
items.why: Students collapse 1917 into one event. The Tsar abdicated in February under pressure from liberals and soldiers — the Bolsheviks took power eight months later from a republican government.

items.q: How long did the Montgomery Bus Boycott last?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 381 days. “100 days” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Montgomery police. “FBI” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was the Civil Rights Act passed?
items.why: The real figure is 1964 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
